curation_notes: Re-curated with updated hint guidelines. The vulnerability is in executor/oci/spec.go: the old `sub` function resolved cache mount subpaths via fs.RootPath without TOCTOU protection, and the `subMount` method did not cache previously resolved submounts, re-resolving the path on every access. This allowed parallel build steps sharing a cache mount to race the path resolution and substitute symlinks between validation and mount. The patch moved `sub` to platform-specific files with fd-based verification and retry logic on Linux, added submount reference caching in `subMount`, and introduced a ForceRemount option in the snapshot package. 7 non-test source files were changed across executor/oci and snapshot packages, so noisy_patch is true. L3 hints narrowed to OCI spec generation for container mounts within the executor layer. Vulnerability class race-condition confirmed from CWE-362 and the TOCTOU nature of the fix.
